debug_info.goal:
Ident : Type u_1
ε : @Graphiti.FinEnv Ident (String × String)
a : List String
⊢ ∃ b,
    a.length = b.length ∧
      (∀ (a' b' : String), (a', b') ∈ a.zip b → ¬Batteries.AssocList.contains (a', b') ε = true) ∧ (a.zip b).Nodup

failed_attempts:
let b := List.range a.length |>.map fun i => toString i
  refine' ⟨b, _, _, _⟩
  · simp [b]
  · intro a' b' h
    simp_all [Batteries.AssocList.contains, List.mem_zip]
    intro h'
    have := List.Nodup.of_assoc (List.map (fun i => (toString i, toString i)) (List.range a.length))
    simp_all
  · apply List.Nodup.zip
    · apply List.Nodup.of_injective
      intro i j h
      simp_all
    · apply List.Nodup.of_injective
      intro i j h
      simp_all

have : ∀ n : Nat, ∃ b : List String, a.length = b.length ∧ (∀ a' b', (a', b') ∈ a.zip b → ¬ ε.contains (a', b')) ∧ (a.zip b).Nodup := by
    intro n
    let b := List.range a.length |>.map fun i => toString i
    refine' ⟨b, rfl, _, _⟩
    · intro a' b' h
      simp_all [List.mem_zip, List.range]
      intro h'
      revert h'
      simp_all [Batteries.AssocList.contains, List.find?]
    · simp_all [List.Nodup, List.mem_zip, List.range]
  exact this 0

have : ∀ l : List String, ∃ b : List String,
    l.length = b.length ∧ (∀ a' b', (a', b') ∈ l.zip b → ¬ ε.contains (a', b')) ∧ (l.zip b).Nodup := by
    intro l
    induction l with
    | nil => exact ⟨[], by simp⟩
    | cons x xs ih =>
      obtain ⟨b, hb1, hb2, hb3⟩ := ih
      have : ∃ y : String, ¬ ε.contains (x, y) ∧ ∀ z ∈ b, y ≠ z := by
        -- Since strings are infinite and ε is finite, we can always find such a y
        let used := {y : String | ε.contains (x, y)}
        let used2 := {y : String | y ∈ b}
        have hused : Set.Finite used := by
          apply Set.finite_subset (Set.finite_image Prod.snd (Finset.finite_to_set _))
          rintro y hy
          exact ⟨(x, y), hy, rfl⟩
        have hused2 : Set.Finite used2 := Set.finite_mem_finset (Finset.finite_to_set _)
        have : Set.Infinite (Set.univ : Set String) := by
          apply Set.infinite_univ
        have : Set.Infinite (Set.univ \ (used ∪ used2)) := by
          apply Set.infinite_diff
          · exact this
          · exact Set.finite_union hused hused2
        obtain ⟨y, hy⟩ := Set.exists_mem_of_infinite this
        exact ⟨y, by simpa using hy.1, fun z hz => by simpa using hy.2.resolve_left hz⟩
      obtain ⟨y, hy1, hy2⟩ := this
      refine' ⟨y :: b, _, _, _⟩
      · simp [hb1]
      · intros a' b' h
        cases h with
        | head => exact hy1
        | tail h' => exact hb2 _ _ h'
      · simp [hb3, hy2]
  exact this a

have : ∀ n : Nat, ∃ b : List String, a.length = b.length ∧ (∀ a' b', (a', b') ∈ a.zip b → ¬ ε.contains (a', b')) ∧ (a.zip b).Nodup := by
    intro n
    let b := List.range a.length |>.map (fun i => toString i)
    refine' ⟨b, _, _, _⟩
    · rfl
    · intro a' b' h
      simp_all [List.mem_zip, List.range]
      intro h'
      revert h'
      simp_all [Batteries.AssocList.contains, List.find?]
      omega
    · apply List.Nodup.zip_left
      apply List.Nodup.map
      apply List.Nodup.range
  exact this 0

have : ∀ l : List String, ∃ b : List String,
    l.length = b.length ∧ (∀ a' b', (a', b') ∈ l.zip b → ¬ ε.contains (a', b')) ∧ (l.zip b).Nodup := by
    intro l
    induction l with
    | nil => exact ⟨[], by simp⟩
    | cons x xs ih =>
      obtain ⟨b, hb1, hb2, hb3⟩ := ih
      have : ∃ y : String, ¬ ε.contains (x, y) ∧ ∀ z ∈ b, y ≠ z := by
        -- Since strings are infinite and ε is finite, we can always find such a y
        let used := ε.keys.map Prod.snd ++ b
        have : ∃ y : String, y ∉ used := by
          apply Classical.not_forall_not.mp
          intro h
          have : Set.univ ⊆ used.toFinset.toSet := by
            intro y hy
            exact absurd hy (h y)
          exact Set.toFinset_toSet_eq_of_finite (Set.finite_univ) this
        obtain ⟨y, hy⟩ := this
        refine ⟨y, ?_, ?_⟩
        · intro hcont
          have : y ∈ used := by
            simp only [used, List.mem_append, List.mem_map, Prod.exists, exists_and_right] at *
            aesop
          contradiction
        · intro z hz
          have : y ∉ used := hy
          simp only [used, List.mem_append, List.mem_map, Prod.exists, exists_and_right] at *
          aesop
      obtain ⟨y, hy1, hy2⟩ := this
      refine ⟨y :: b, ?_, ?_, ?_⟩
      · simp [hb1]
      · intro a' b' hmem
        cases hmem with
        | head => exact hy1
        | tail h => exact hb2 _ _ h
      · simp [hb3, hy2]
  exact this a

have : ∀ l : List String, ∃ b : List String,
    l.length = b.length ∧ (∀ a' b', (a', b') ∈ l.zip b → ¬ ε.contains (a', b')) ∧ (l.zip b).Nodup := by
    intro l
    induction l with
    | nil => exact ⟨[], by simp⟩
    | cons x xs ih =>
      obtain ⟨b, hb1, hb2, hb3⟩ := ih
      have : ∃ y : String, ¬ ε.contains (x, y) ∧ ∀ z ∈ b, y ≠ z := by
        -- Since strings are infinite and ε is finite, we can always find such a y
        let used := ε.keys.map Prod.snd ++ b
        have : ∃ y : String, y ∉ used := by
          -- Use that String is infinite; pick a fresh string not in the finite list `used`
          apply Classical.choose_spec
          apply Classical.em
          apply Set.finite.exists_not_mem
          exact Set.finite.of_finite_subset (Set.finite_union (Set.finite_image _ ε.toSet.finite) (Set.finite_mem_finset _))
        obtain ⟨y, hy⟩ := this
        exact ⟨y, by
          simp only [Batteries.AssocList.contains, FinEnv.toEnv, AssocList.find?]
          intro h
          apply hy
          simp_all [List.mem_map, Prod.exists, List.mem_append]
          , by
          intro z hz
          apply hy
          simp_all [List.mem_append]⟩
      obtain ⟨y, hy1, hy2⟩ := this
      refine ⟨y :: b, ?_, ?_, ?_⟩
      · simp [hb1]
      · intro a' b' h
        cases h with
        | head => exact hy1
        | tail h' =>
          apply hb2
          exact h'
      · simp [hb3, hy2]
  exact this a

    · apply List.Nodup.of_nodup_map

have : ∀ l : List String, ∃ b : List String,
    l.length = b.length ∧ (∀ a' b', (a', b') ∈ l.zip b → ¬ ε.contains (a', b')) ∧ (l.zip b).Nodup := by
    intro l
    induction l with
    | nil => exact ⟨[], by simp⟩
    | cons x xs ih =>
      obtain ⟨b, hb1, hb2, hb3⟩ := ih
      have : ∃ y : String, ¬ ε.contains (x, y) ∧ ∀ z ∈ b, y ≠ z := by
        -- Since strings are infinite and ε is finite, we can always find such a y
        have : {y : String | ε.contains (x, y)}ᶜ.Nonempty := by
          apply Set.nonempty_compl_of_not_mem
          intro h
          have : ε.toList.length < Nat.succ (ε.toList.length) := Nat.lt_succ_self _
          -- But there are infinitely many strings, so the set of y with (x,y) in ε is finite
          -- Hence its complement is nonempty
          sorry
        sorry
      sorry
  exact this a

    l.length = b.length ∧ (∀ a' b', (a', b') ∈ l.zip b → ¬ ε.contains (a', b')) ∧ (l.zip b).Nodup := by

    induction l with
    | nil => exact ⟨[], by simp⟩
    | cons x xs ih =>
      obtain ⟨b, hb1, hb2, hb3⟩ := ih
      have : ∃ y : String, ¬ ε.contains (x, y) ∧ ∀ z ∈ b, y ≠ z := by
        -- Since strings are infinite and ε is finite, we can always find such a y
        sorry
      obtain ⟨y, hy1, hy2⟩ := this
      refine ⟨y :: b, ?_, ?_, ?_⟩
      · simp [hb1]
      · intro a' b' h
        cases h with
        | head => exact hy1
        | tail h' => exact hb2 _ _ h'
      · simp [hb3, hy2]
  exact this a

      have : ∃ y : String, ¬ ε.contains (x, y) ∧ ∀ z ∈ b, y ≠ z := by
        -- Since strings are infinite and ε is finite, we can always find such a y
        sorry

      refine' ⟨y :: b, _, _, _⟩ <;> simp_all [List.zip, List.Nodup, List.mem_cons]

refine' ⟨List.map (fun _ => "") a, _, _, _⟩ <;> simp [List.length_map]
  <;> intro a' b' h <;> simp_all [List.mem_zip, List.Nodup]

have : ∀ n : Nat, ∃ b : List String, a.length = b.length ∧ (∀ a' b', (a', b') ∈ a.zip b → ¬ ε.contains (a', b')) ∧ (a.zip b).Nodup := by
    intro n
    let b := List.range a.length |>.map (fun i => toString i)
    refine' ⟨b, rfl, _, _⟩
    · intro a' b' h
      simp_all [List.mem_zip, List.range]
      intro h'
      exact absurd h' (by
        simp_all [Batteries.AssocList.contains, List.find?]
        apply List.find?_eq_none_of_forall_not
        intro p _
        cases p <;> simp_all)
    · simp_all [List.Nodup, List.range]
  exact this 0

have : ∀ l : List String, ∃ b : List String,
    l.length = b.length ∧ (∀ a' b', (a', b') ∈ l.zip b → ¬ ε.contains (a', b')) ∧ (l.zip b).Nodup := by
    intro l
    induction l with
    | nil => exact ⟨[], by simp⟩
    | cons x xs ih =>
      obtain ⟨b, hb1, hb2, hb3⟩ := ih
      have : ∃ y : String, ¬ ε.contains (x, y) ∧ ∀ z ∈ b, y ≠ z := by
        -- Since strings are infinite and ε is finite, we can always find such a y
        -- We use the fact that there are infinitely many strings but only finitely many in ε
        -- For simplicity, we construct a string not in the finite set of second components
        let used := ε.toList.map Prod.snd ++ b
        have : ∃ y : String, y ∉ used := by
          -- There are infinitely many strings, so we can always find one not in a finite list
          -- We use a simple diagonalization: append 'x' repeatedly until we get a fresh one
          refine' ⟨String.mk (List.replicate (used.length + 1) 'x'), _⟩
          simp [List.mem_map, List.mem_append]
          intro h
          cases h with
          | inl h =>
            simp_all [List.mem_map]
            intro i hi
            simp_all [List.get?_eq_getElem, List.getElem_replicate]
          | inr h =>
            simp_all [List.mem_replicate]
      obtain ⟨y, hy1, hy2⟩ := this
      refine' ⟨y :: b, _, _, _⟩
      · simp [hb1]
      · intro a' b' h
        cases h with
        | head => exact hy1
        | tail h =>
          apply hb2
          exact h
      · simp [hb3, hy2]
  exact this a

have : ∀ n : Nat, ∃ b : List String, a.length = b.length ∧ (∀ a' b', (a', b') ∈ a.zip b → ¬ ε.contains (a', b')) ∧ (a.zip b).Nodup := by
    intro n
    let b := List.range a.length |>.map (fun i => toString i)
    refine' ⟨b, rfl, _, _⟩
    · intro a' b' h
      simp_all [List.mem_zip, List.map]
      intro i hi1 hi2
      simp_all [Batteries.AssocList.contains, List.find?]
      aesop
    · simp_all [List.Nodup, List.map]
      apply List.Nodup.of_pairwise
      simp_all [List.Pairwise, List.map]
      intros i j hi hj h
      simp_all
  exact this 0

refine' ⟨List.map (fun _ => "fresh") a, _, _, _⟩
  · simp
  · intro a' b' h
    simp_all [Batteries.AssocList.contains]
  · simp [List.Nodup]

have : ∀ l : List String, ∃ b : List String,
    l.length = b.length ∧ (∀ a' b', (a', b') ∈ l.zip b → ¬ ε.contains (a', b')) ∧ (l.zip b).Nodup := by
    intro l
    induction l with
    | nil => exact ⟨[], by simp⟩
    | cons x xs ih =>
      obtain ⟨b, hb1, hb2, hb3⟩ := ih
      have : ∃ y : String, ¬ ε.contains (x, y) ∧ ∀ z ∈ b, y ≠ z := by
        -- Since strings are infinite and ε is finite, we can always find such a y
        let S := {y : String | ε.contains (x, y) ∨ y ∈ b}
        have hS : Set.Finite S := by
          apply Set.finite_union
          · apply Set.finite_to_set
            apply List.toFinset_finite
            exact (ε.toList.filter (fun p => p.1.1 = x)).map (·.1.2)
          · exact Set.finite_mem_finset (b.toFinset)
        have hinf : Infinite String := inferInstance
        have hnot : ¬ Set.Finite (Set.univ : Set String) := by
          exact hinf.not_finite
        have hnotin : ∃ y, y ∉ S := by
          apply Set.exists_not_mem_of_finite_of_infinite hS hinf
          exact Set.univ_infinite
        obtain ⟨y, hy⟩ := hnotin
        exact ⟨y, by simp [Set.mem_union, Set.mem_setOf_eq] at hy; tauto⟩
      obtain ⟨y, hy1, hy2⟩ := this
      refine ⟨y :: b, ?_, ?_, ?_⟩
      · simp [hb1]
      · intro a' b' hmem
        cases hmem with
        | head h => simp_all
        | tail h => simp_all; apply hb2; assumption
      · simp [List.Nodup.cons, hb3, hy2]
  exact this a
